title: Eataly
name: Jeff Cooper
text: Really enjoy this experience for a laid back, extremely fresh and well serviced meal.  While there are many elegant Italian places you can eat in Manhattan, Eataly provides a fun atmosphere, great service and as authentic of food as you will find.  Would definitely come back

title: Eataly
name: Diana Cardona
text:
We visited Eataly NYC Downtown - La Pizza & La Pasta to celebrate my daughter’s 12th birthday with a party of nine this Saturday. We had a 3:00 PM reservation and arrived a little early. I informed the hostess that we had purchased a birthday cake from Eataly’s pastry counter to bring out at the end of the meal, and she said it would not be a problem.
Our server Patricia was attentive. When the meal ended, she politely informed me there would be a cake-cutting fee, which I immediately agreed to. That was completely reasonable.
Unfortunately, the experience was disrupted by a manager named Simon. As I was placing candles on the cake, he abruptly approached and said, “You can’t bring outside food, you will be charged.” I explained that Patricia had already informed me and that I had agreed to the fee. I also expressed that I did not appreciate his tone and suggested that he could take a cue from Patricia’s courteous and professional way of communicating with guests.
Simon then waved his hand dismissively and announced, “I am the manager and you will be charged.” I again reiterated that I had no objection to the fee whatsoever; my concern was solely with his tone and approach. He responded by waving his hand dismissively in my face and telling me to “just relax.”
When you are paying for a full meal for nine people to celebrate a child’s birthday, a cake fee is nominal in comparison to the bill. The fee was never the issue—his rude and arrogant manner was.
Perhaps Simon is accustomed to dealing with tourists and believes this behavior is acceptable. It is not. His conduct created tension that was felt by everyone at the table and overshadowed what should have been a joyful family celebration.
Patricia provided attentive service, but the behavior from Simon as management at Eataly NYC Downtown - La Pizza & La Pasta was unprofessional and disappointing. I hope management reviews this incident so future guests celebrating special occasions have a better experience. Simon would benefit from additional training in guest relations and professional communication.

title: Eataly
name: Mark
text:
Always the best of Italy ...
Great Sales & Deal

title: Eataly
name: Maria Camila Garnica
text: Llegamos a la 9 pm y nos dijeron que en 10 min cerraban..? Era obvio que no nos querían atender porque en los horarios dice que cierran a las 11 pm, so…?

title: Eataly
name: Robin F
text: I took a tortellini class with Riccardo and Patty, and it was great! They were super entertaining and educational and now I can make delicious pasta myself, I definitely recommend taking their class!

title: Eataly
name: Jennifer Maria
text: We had dinner at Vino and the service was honestly disappointing. There was no attentiveness at all, if you had a question, no one seemed interested in helping. At one point  we literally had to hand our plates to a support staff member ourselves. Our server only came to the table when we asked for the check and even it took 15 minutes to pay. Mandatory 20% tip for a service like this? ?  ?

title: Eataly
name: Maurice Boucher
text:
I’ve been to Eataly 3–4 times already, and it never disappoints.

What I love is that you actually hear Italian spoken there — many of the staff are Italian, which adds to the authenticity of the whole experience. The ambiance feels lively and genuinely European, and the quality of the food consistently delivers.

I highly recommend the tortellini — I enjoyed every single bite. It’s the quintessence of what good pasta should be: simple, balanced, and deeply satisfying.

The view over the city makes the whole experience even better.

Enjoy every bite. 🇮🇹🍝

title: Eataly
name: Marcos Campanati
text:
I’ve always associated Eataly with warmth, Italian hospitality, and a joyful dining experience. Unfortunately, that was not our experience today.

The service felt cold and unwelcoming, lacking the kindness and attentiveness one would expect from a place that celebrates Italian culture. The food was good, but the atmosphere created by the staff made the experience feel transactional rather than enjoyable.

Hospitality is as important as the cuisine, and today that element was missing.

title: Eataly
name: Ludo Vico
text: We took a cooking class making tortellini and it was phenomenal!! The teacher Ricardo was so much fun and educational. His charming accent and personality made our night. Patty the Deus ex machina was the real boss. Grazi

title: Eataly
name: Lena Gueldner
text: We took a pasta cooking class and it was absolutely amazing. The chef Riccardo Costa was super friendly and knowledgeable and he truly made our night!! He had so many little stories to tell and great Italian cooking expertise, I‘ll definitely incorporate many of the cooking life hacks he showed us into my cooking! The food and wine was also great — shoutout also to his friendly coworker Patty who helped to support all of us learners during the class! They really made an unforgettable night for us 😊😊 Great as a date, but also if you‘re just with friends :)))

title: Eataly
name: Sophie Gartmann
text:
We had an absolutely fantastic cooking class at Eataly on Liberty Street in New York! A huge thank you to Chef Riccardo Costa and his wonderful coworker Patty for such an unforgettable experience.
During the class, we learned how to make fresh pasta from scratch, and it was truly amazing. Chef Riccardo explained every step with great passion and professionalism. You can immediately feel how much he loves Italian cuisine, and he made the whole experience both fun and educational.

title: Eataly
name: Ivan Galiano
text: Eataly estuvo excelente, una buena experiencia, comida y bebida muy recomendable. Vale la pena

title: Eataly
name: Gabriele Momoli
text: Abbastanza bene ma avrei pensato molto meglio visto il blasone. Personale non proprio preparato. 100 euro per 3 pizze a New York ci stanno, ma devono essere fatte bene. Ci hanno fatto sedere nell’area comune senza dirci dei tavoli al ristorante.

title: Eataly
name: Sonia Dey
text: Wow. Both the dishes were fantastic. The pizza was cheesy and the fettuccine was so buttery. I literally scooped the last bit of that truffle butter with a bread. Highly recommended. Will visit again.

title: Eataly
name: Alicia C.
text: I absolutely love this place! The ambience is so upbeat! So may people walking around shopping or simply enjoying a glass of wine as they are shopping. The main issue I had it was that I couldn’t make up my mind on what to eat. I have been here so many times and yet, I always go through this. I ended up buying a pasta dish and sat by myself in a quieter area. I had the company of a tiny bird who seemed so out of place here, but nevertheless I truly enjoyed his company.

title: Eataly
name: Toby Shea
text: I can’t believe Eataly is serving day old bread. This JFK airport location does not have the same quality.

title: Eataly
name: Mechteld van Heuven
text: Al restaurante le falta ambiente, es demasiado grande, muy de conida rapida. La pizza estaba muy buena pero pedimos una pasta de 39 dolares que no es barato y estaba malisimo. Estaba seco y todas las pastas estaban pegadas.

title: Eataly
name: Billy
text:
Eataly is a beautiful place, but to be honest, the food is not that good. I had lasagna tonight, it was just not good. I can get better at a cheaper place.

This was our second time here, and I don't plan to return.

title: Eataly
name: Pilar Cortez
text: I recently visited Eataly for the first time, and I was amazed by the experience. It’s like stepping into Italy itself. The deli counter is full of authentic cheeses and meats, and the freshly made pasta is to die for. I can’t wait to return and try more!

title: Eataly
name: Jay Bernadette
text: The experience at Eataly World Trade Center was amazing.

title: Eataly
name: Autumn The Nomad
text:
What really stood out here was the atmosphere. The lighting, music, and layout created a space that felt lively without being overwhelming. It was easy to enjoy conversation and stay present.

Nothing felt rushed or disorganized, and the staff helped maintain that relaxed energy throughout the visit.

As a travel streamer, I’m always paying attention to places that feel memorable for the right reasons.
Follow me on Instagram @AutumnTheNomad

title: Eataly
name: Marek Ciesielski
text: Piękna restauracja z widokiem na WTC. Za to oczywiście sie płaci. Jedliśmy tam pizze i paste. Rachunek trochę nas zaskoczył... ale to jest NY...

title: Eataly
name: Artur Morgado
text: Nice store. A lot of Italian options and great food.

title: Eataly
name: Akop Vardanian
text: Hosted

title: Eataly
name: Ethan Reynolds
text: Best Italian I’ve visited in NYC. Service, food and view were awesome. Then, you can shop for stuff! Sweet.

title: Eataly
name: Junior Flores
text: Restaurante con una vista privilegiada, comida deliciosa, un deleite para elmpaladar, ambiente 100% Italiano, niños felices por la comida, además, está rodeado de otros comercios, por ejemplo, sales y puedes comer helados, repostería o frutas frescas

title: Eataly
name: Mark Simson
text: I bought a fruit box on January 20th with an expiration date of January 22nd, but all the fruits were sour, showing clear signs of fermentation and spoilage. Both kiwi and strawberries were rotten, forcing me to throw away the entire box. For a store with such a premium reputation and prices, this is completely unacceptable.

title: Eataly
name: Dario Marinozzi
text: Awesome place to eat and drink very good coffee.

title: Eataly
name: Giulia Poluzzi
text: Dopo la visita di tutta l'area del WTC siamo andate qui a mangiare. Eataly è una garanzia. Noi abbiamo preso ciò che ci andava e poi ci si siamo sedute nei posti a disposizione. Tutto ottimo. Anche il caffè Lavazza è buonissimo. Se volete pranzare, anche con la pizza, la vista del ristorante su tutta l'area è impagabile. È un posto che vi consiglio.

title: Eataly
name: Annie C.
text: Overpriced pizza (it’s not even that good) and the cashier’s attitude was awful.

title: Eataly
name: iris b
text: We stopped here for a short break and were lucky to find a table! The baking goods were fresh and tasty , the coffee a little less….

title: Eataly
name: Léa
text:
I visited Eataly recently and overall had a pleasant experience, though with mixed feelings. The place is large and lively, combining an Italian market with several food counters — pasta bar, gelato, pastries, coffee, and ready-to-eat options — which makes it convenient and appealing at first glance.

I had a small salmon sandwich that cost $12, and while it was good, I personally found it a bit overpriced for the portion and quality. In general, prices felt quite high compared to what you actually get.

That said, the atmosphere is nice and enjoyable, especially if you’re stopping by while exploring the area. It’s a comfortable place to sit, take a break, and grab something quickly. However, it does feel more tourist-oriented than truly authentic Italian.

Overall, it’s a good option for convenience and variety, but not necessarily for an authentic Italian food experience.

title: Eataly
name: mitchell fuchs
text: Had the fettuccine Alfredo made infront of us. It was stellar.

title: Eataly
name: Adeline Rawlings
text: Italian grocery and restaurant in one store, look no further than Eataly! Eataly's Lower Manhattan location in the Oculus serves some of the best Italian pizza in NYC and pasta here is super fresh both in the restaurant and in store. Eataly is filled with a selection of specialties in their café such as cappuccinos, latte drinks, and pastries. Definitely recommending shopping around as they carry Davines hair products, a variety of Italian pastas, and fresh produce! In walking distance to the Oculus' train station

title: Eataly
name: JORGE ZARATE
text: La mejor pasta con Langosta que he comido en mi vida.

title: Eataly
name: Jessica Vu
text:
My partner and I dined here during our holiday trip to New York before Christmas. While the food was excellent, the service and atmosphere were unprofessional and disappointing.

One staff member in particular, repeatedly whispered and laughed with coworkers while looking directly at us, which made us feel judged for how we were dining. He was not our waiter, but he was responsible for clearing crumbs and plates off of our table.

This behavior ruined our meal and spoiled what should have been a special part of our trip. We regret spending so much here—the atmosphere was unprofessional and far below the quality or price of the food. We will not be coming back.

title: Eataly
name: Wendy Alvarenga
text: Food is good but being charged 20% gratuity on a 2 top is unacceptable from someone who has been in the industry for over 5 years this is insane specially when we didn’t get no service at all just someone who took the order and a different waiter dropping the check.

title: Eataly

text: we weren’t dine here, however, we got a tiramisu, the price of is 20 bucks for not so reasonable size, but I was craving for that. But it was so bad especially the ladyfinger cookie does not have rum in it or coffee. When I ask the person who sales it, he said there was coffee in there. I told him this tastes not authentic and didn’t ask for a refund, his attitude were shocking and not responsive. it was such a disappointment.

title: Eataly

title: Eataly

text: El lugar es precioso, tienen una muy buena atención, los meseros son muy atentos en especial Steve y Gerardo, la pasta es muy rica y los profiteroles de postre 10/10.

title: Eataly
name: TCP
text: Wow! Kind of like Whole Foods and Harrods had a baby! Did not even know where to start - everything looked so good I could have literally eaten there everyday for a week but we were just there on a visit to the 9/11 Memorial. We tried the pizzas, pastries and gelato all great. 😊

title: Eataly
name: Amin Asgharzadeh
text:
It’s always refreshing to visit Eataly, especially when you finally find what you’ve been searching for! We have been looking forward to trying their famous pistachio croissant for a long time. After visiting two other Eataly locations without luck (they likely sold out before we arrived), we finally found them here.
I can’t recommend this croissant enough! It’s definitely on the heavier side—it could practically replace a lunch in terms of calories—but it is totally worth it. Pro tip: Try to go in the morning, otherwise they will likely be sold out!

title: Eataly
name: Mowgli Boardef
text: Endroit sympa mais service innaceptable , limite nous les dérangeons , le service est inconcevable… aucun sourire fin bref

title: Eataly
name: Pierre-Marie Chaillet
text: Great place, great food but very bad service at the bar and focaccia stand. Service was realy bad, empoye speaking bad to customers and dont expect any smile.

title: Eataly
name: R Garcia
text: We ordered fritto misto and bruschetta for apps. Pastas and pizzas for entree. Everything was soooo good! Portioning is just right. Pizza crust was light. Kids loved the pastas we had no leftovers.

title: Eataly
name: Brianna
text: One of the female servers was rude and rolled her eyes and walked away. All we told her was that we didn’t have a menu. It’s also very crowded and the food isn’t worth the hype:

title: Eataly
name: Mitre Krajoski
text:
I’ve been here several times, mainly because I stay at a hotel just across the street. Every visit, getting a table is difficult. I’ve tried breakfast, brunch, and lunch.

The food quality is excellent—really very good—but the prices are extremely high and, in my opinion, overpriced for what you get. Unfortunately, the overall experience doesn’t match the quality of the food.

The place is very busy and noisy, making it hard to relax and enjoy the meal. The staff are not very polite or communicative, and I never experienced a welcoming attitude or even a smile.

One particularly annoying experience was when I ordered a pizza for takeaway. The pizza was already prepared and sitting in front of me, but because the staff member I ordered from wasn’t there, I was forced to wait another 10 minutes just for confirmation—even though there was only one pizza and one order.

Because of the high prices, noisy atmosphere, and poor service, despite the excellent food quality, I would rate this place as average

title: Eataly
name: Celestica
text: Very Good atmosphere, calm place to go eat in family, I like the variety of options to eat and very Italian. The team is amazing and friendly! Definitely recommend this Eataly and will definitely come back 🙌

title: Eataly
name: Canada Travel
text: Amazing place to stop by and have breakfast , , ,close to 9/11 memorial !! The best iced cappuccino in the town !

title: Eataly

text:
개인적으론 로마에서 먹은 피자급으로 맛있었어요.
생면 파스타 처음 보는데 파스타도 입맛에 잘 맞았고 음식점 분위기도 매우 좋았습니다👍

title: Eataly
name: Arselan Benbekhouche (Arselan Benbekhouche)
text: Le beignet était pas incroyable, il y avait quasiment pas de crème à l’intérieur pour 7$ je trouve ça abusé

title: Eataly
name: Michel Galvis
text: Best Italian experience and the food was so good! I highly recommend it! Must to stop by 😮‍💨

title: Eataly
name: Wow Brand Relations
text:
We arrived 15 minutes before closing and were told we could only order one dish, even though my young daughter was starving.
We already had other food to take back to the hotel, yet we were repeatedly met with complaints about the restaurant closing.
I understand closing procedures, but the attitude and manners were unacceptable.
Beautiful place and great food, but very poor service.

title: Eataly
name: Rishi Mehta
text: Poor service, food is extremely bland and you can’t even change it because they have a policy that the chef’s recipe cannot be changed. Well they need to tell their chef to actually cook good food in the first place then.

title: Eataly
name: Kriti Gautam
text: The worst experience of my life. The waiter was all jokes no humor and felt like he was looking down upon us. First off when you reserve a table it doesn’t mention which place you are reserving it. We were already caught off guard when that happened and then the waiter was the worst. Overall would never go back again

title: Eataly
name: paddington matz
text: My favorite pizza in New York city paper thin puffy crust with a little bit of crunch in a lot of chew is so delicious when paired with the arugula, lemon vinaigrette, shaved Parmesan salad together it’s an outstanding combination

title: Eataly
name: Mark Falcón
text: Justo luego de realizar el recorrido del museo del 9/11, muy cerca de allí está este super restaurante. También tiene una zona de compras, pero aquí lo importante es su restaurante, de excelente servicio, muy buena selección de pastas y vinos. Como el restaurante esta en el último piso del edificio, tiene una muy buena vista de la ciudad. Super Ultra Recomendable!

title: Eataly
name: Kendra Shepherd
text: We always love coming here when we’re in town. Never disappoints and the food and service are always great!

title: Eataly
name: Cinthia Menutole
text:
It used to be goog when it opened. I went yesterday for caffe with panna, was served a burned espresso with bad whipped cream. Cakes look old.
Go have your pastries and coffee at San Ambreus at the Oculus, much better than this store, with a bad coffee and old pastries.

title: Eataly
name: R D
text:
Eataly NYC at the Oculus has, unfortunately, become a consistently disappointing destination, with quality that seems to decline with every visit. For a venue positioned as a premium Italian food hall in such a prominent and expensive location, the overall experience falls well short of expectations.
Food quality and execution. Also the pizza arrived cold.
The pizza is presented on a  metal plate, covered by paper. Which feels more cafeteria than curated Italian dining and does not align with the upscale image or pricing of the establishment. The dough itself is chewy and lacks depth of flavor, weak execution for a dish that should be a cornerstone of any serious Italian offering.
Burrata
The burrata, traditionally prized for its soft, creamy center, was served unusually firm and dense, closer in texture to a standard mozzarella than to a fresh burrata. For a brand that markets itself on high-quality Italian ingredients, serving a burrata in this condition raises concerns about both product handling and quality control.
Value versus expectations
Given the premium pricing and the high-traffic World Trade Center / Oculus location, guests are entitled to expect a higher standard of culinary consistency and presentation. Instead, the experience feels misaligned with the cost, leaving the impression of a concept trading on its name

title: Eataly
name: Crank94
text: Leider das schlechteste Pizza, welche wir gegessen haben. Vom verbrannten Teig waren auch die Zutaten geschmacklos. Ich rate es nicht.

title: Eataly
name: Christopher Sledz
text: I went out with my gf the waiter saw we were younger did not provide proper service at all and FORCED A 20 dollar tip as we tried to give only 10 because the service was horrendous also the food was not that good either

title: Eataly
name: Michael William Majorczak
text: So many choices. Just amazing. Great place for coffee and desert.

title: Eataly
name: Bebe Phivilay
text:
Eataly is an incredible Italian food destination that blends dining, shopping, and culture all in one place. The quality of ingredients really stands out—from fresh pasta and wood-fired pizza to imported cheeses, cured meats, and excellent espresso. The market is fun to browse, with so many authentic Italian products and wines you won’t find everywhere.

The restaurants and food counters offer something for every craving, whether you want a full sit-down meal or a quick bite. It can get busy and prices are on the higher side, but the freshness, authenticity, and overall experience make it worth it. A must-visit for Italian food lovers.

title: Eataly
name: Thi
text: Killer gelatos

title: Eataly
name: Steven Devitt
text: Underwhelming experience.  Nice atmosphere but the food was very average.

title: Eataly
name: Jose Orlando
text:
Came specifically for this location, only to find a sign at the entrance saying “Closed due to private event.”
No advance notice on Google Maps, no website update, no warning until you physically arrive at the door.

This is not a small café — Eataly is a destination venue. Closing for a private event is something planned in advance, and customers should not have their time wasted like this.

If you plan to shut down regular service, at least communicate it clearly online.
Very disappointing experience and poor customer consideration.

title: Eataly
name: Ardak MUA
text:
Disappointing Experience with Management

Before we even had a chance to sit down and enjoy our food, we experienced extremely poor customer service at the main Eataly restaurant on the 2nd floor at 101 Liberty St, New York, NY 10007.

It was around 4 PM on a Monday 12/15/2025 not a busy time at all. When we exited the elevator, the restaurant and hostess stand were directly in front of us. A manager standing there immediately yelled, “Stay right there, I’ll come to you” no greeting, no welcome.

We were a group of 7 people (4 adults, 1 teen, and 2 small children). She insisted we should go to Al Pasta & Al Pizza because they have a kids menu and the main restaurant does not. I explained that we know the menu well and that it would still work for us, and that we were fine ordering regular pasta for the kids. She kept repeating that there is no kids menu, even after I clearly said that was not an issue for us.

Shortly after, an older couple walked in and were seated immediately, while we were told to wait. While seating them, the manager looked at me and pointed at my stroller and said, “Can you put that thing over there?” That was the final straw for me. We decided to leave.

We went to Al Pasta & Al Pizza, which is also part of Eataly but on the other side, and the experience there was the complete opposite. The hostess was warm and kind, greeted all of our kids, and you could tell she genuinely loves children. Our waitress was wonderful, and the food and service were excellent.

When we were leaving and walking back toward the elevator, I wanted to ask for the manager’s name. The hostess said she could not give it to me and called her instead. When I politely asked for her name, she again was rude and refused to share it, saying she manages multiple locations. I find it unacceptable for someone in a management position to refuse to identify themselves when asked.

I have lived in New York for many years and have never experienced this type of behavior, nor am I someone who leaves negative reviews, this is actually my first one. I’m sharing this so families with kids are aware. Based on this experience, this location, or at least the management we encountered, is clearly not kid-friendly…

title: Eataly
name: D.N.M.
text:
Eataly New York is a great place to experience authentic Italian food in the heart of the city. During our visit, we tried several traditional dishes and everything was delicious.
We tasted different pizzas, which were well cooked, flavorful, and made with high-quality ingredients. The dough was light and crispy, just like in Italy. We also had rustic bread served with olive oil, which was excellent and very fragrant, with a rich and genuine taste.
Another highlight was the Italian-style potatoes, simple but perfectly prepared, with a homemade flavor that reminded us of traditional Italian cuisine. The quality of the ingredients really stood out in every dish.
The atmosphere is lively and welcoming, and the place is well organized despite being quite busy. It’s a perfect spot for a casual meal or to try real Italian flavors while visiting New York.
So, offers an authentic Italian food experience with excellent quality and a warm atmosphere. Highly recommended for anyone who loves Italian cuisine.

title: Eataly
name: Sean Devlin
text: Just a fun spot to grab dinner or grab some good Italian food to prep at home!

title: Eataly
name: Kunal Sharma
text:
I tried to order a Cannolo with cherries and orange (their special).

Out of orange — so I agree to just cherries. They try to ring it up as something different (and 50 cents extra). So they want to charge me extra because they’re out of an ingredient.

title: Eataly
name: Kate
text: Best Tiramisu

title: Eataly
name: Lake Lalande
text: Food is delicious and our waiter Ronnie is exceptional. Wonderful atmosphere and beautiful views.

title: Eataly
name: Lisa Fischer
text: Ronnie was the best...  ever!

title: Eataly
name: Jacqueline Dumouchelle
text: We had the most amazing server RONNIE!!!!

title: Eataly
name: diogo spiandorello
text: Eataly is an absolute must-visit! From the moment I walked in, the hospitality was top-notch specially by Ronny one of the people who made the night. What really blew me away was the personal touch – they sent a birthday card! It’s these little things that make a huge difference. And let’s not forget the food; every single bite was amazing and delicious. The quality of the ingredients shines through in every dish. If you're looking for an authentic Italian experience with incredible service and food, Eataly is the place to go. Highly recommended! I had the capricciosa pizza , which is a must go if you like anchovies ‼️

title: Eataly
name: Justine
text:
Went here for my partner’s birthday dinner. I truly want to give this place 5 stars because the food was really good and the atmosphere was phenomenal. Our servers Johnnie and Justin were really accommodating and quick.
The only thing that really ruined the experience for us was the manager/ supervisor.
We’d taken some stuff to go and had already paid our bill, and my partner was in the middle of finishing his drink, when out of nowhere, an older female supervisor rounds the corner and basically scolds us and tells us that they have to set up for a party. No apologies. No warmth whatsoever. She had never interacted with us prior to that moment. She didn’t even give us a chance to respond and just high tailed out of there.
Our interaction with her just ruined what was a pretty good evening and left a bad taste in our mouths. Funny enough, someone else wrote a bad review about her.
Maybe someone could teach her a lesson in hospitality.

title: Eataly
name: ELA Teacher
text: Beautifully decorated and the food was out of this world!

title: Eataly
name: Artography
text:
Eataly at World Trade Center is an easy win when you’re craving a quick pasta fix without committing to a full sit-down meal. For an elevated food-court setting, the execution is excellent: the pasta is freshly made, well-sauced, and consistently flavorful, delivering that comforting Italian simplicity Eataly does so well.

Portions are on the smaller side, but thoughtfully so—just enough to satisfy without leaving you overly full, making it ideal for a lunch break or a pre-errand bite downtown. Everything feels efficient and polished, from ordering to pickup, which matters in a high-traffic location like WTC.

The only real drawback is the price. While the quality justifies it to an extent, it does feel a touch expensive for what is, at the end of the day, an upscale food-court experience.

Final take: Excellent pasta, smart portions, and reliable quality in a convenient location—just a bit pricey. ★★★★☆

title: Eataly
name: Christina Gao
text:
Update: No one has ever reached out to me to address the situation.

I’m very disappointed with my experience at Eataly this Thanksgiving. The service was especially poor compared to my visits last year. Our server was barely present. She took our order at the beginning and then disappeared until it was time to bring the bill. No one checked on us at all.

It took 40 minutes just to receive the antipasti with the first course, and during that entire time, no staff member came by to check in or update us. We simply sat there waiting in silence, unsure of what was happening.

Meanwhile, another staff member was constantly refilling water for every table around us but consistently skipped ours. I understand he may not have been responsible for our section, but this only added to the feeling of being ignored, especially considering the restaurant wasn’t even busy.

The food also fell short of expectations. The beet with walnut antipasti tasted off; the walnuts were oily, as if they had been sitting out for too long. The Brussels sprouts were heavily burnt, black, bitter, and unpleasant. Although they offered to replace the side dish, the second serving wasn’t much better, as shown in the photo.

I’m genuinely disappointed that what should have been a warm Thanksgiving celebration turned into such an unsatisfying experience. I hope the restaurant can take this feedback seriously.

text: The experience at Eataly World Trade Center was amazing. From the service to the food I was super impressed. I’ve never eaten authentic Italian cuisine so this was new to my taste buds. The atmosphere was restaurant dine-in and a fresh market together. So it was a bit nosier than most restaurants, but we could still hear each other. The area where we ate was a tad bit tight but still comfortable. I ordered two pasta dishes, butter squash, ravioli and just regular spaghetti no meat. Both were a phenomenal. I’m not sure exactly what was the name of the dishes of the pasta or the dessert? Hopefully, the pictures will help.

title: Eataly
name: Travel with Andy
text:
Wonderful piece of Italy in America! I’m so jealous of New Yorkers that have this gem of a giant marketplace of rich authentic, and rare Italian products (at least rare food in America).

Highly recommend their Vino & restaurant. Book ahead for one of the best pizza spots and pastas freshly made daily. Also, by booking you get a GREAT view of the city! I just had the best calzone of my life there.

My wife is an Italian and as an Italian speaker myself we both love this place. It’s also often a pleasure to speak Italia to the staff here. We just stopped in for my wife’s birthday, where they even comped us the dessert!

title: Eataly

title: Eataly
name: Courtney Parker
text:
If you are coeliac or have any allergies, DO NOT visit this chain!

When entering, I mentioned I was coeliac. I asked the waiter if I could have the pizza, and they said, "No, we really wouldn't recommend the pizza for coeliacs, but the pasta is safe for coeliacs", I therefore asked if the gluten-free pasta was cooked in separate pasta water from the normal pasta, and I was assured it was.

Within 20 minutes of eating my meal, I was ill. I had to go to the toilet, I felt nauseous, I had cold sweats, I was lightheaded, and I had severe abdominal cramps. I had 100% been given/contaminated by gluten.

I messaged their Instagram page upon my return to double-check that the pasta is cooked separately when gluten-free. I was told it is not and that it is not safe for coeliacs.

I made the company aware of the issues I had and explained how dangerous this was, and the waiter assured me the food was safe for me.

The company said I would be contacted by email or phone call to discuss further, which was on the 16th of October.
On the 27th October, I contacted the company to ask whether I would be getting a phone call or email, and they responded apologising for the delay and that no further time would be wasted before they contact me.
On the 3rd November, I asked for an update, and I still (as of 4th December) have not received a response or further communication from this company.

They clearly do not take allergies or coeliac disease seriously in this establishment, so AVOID!

title: Eataly

text: Leider kann ich gar keine gute Rezension für diesen Betrieb abgeben. Das Personal ist unglaublich gestresst und ich musste mehrmals nachfragen, wenn ich etwas wollte. Die Portionen sind extrem klein und die Verhältnisse sehr teuer. Es ist sehr län ick. Eine typische Massenabfertigung in einem Supermarkt. Dies mag wohl für die Leute aus New York passen aber für Touristen definitiv nicht kein guter Ort um hinzugehen.

text:
We first came in for brunch and the food was fine. The goat cheese omelet was solid, but I liked the greens and vinaigrette on the side more than the omelet. The breakfast sandwich was really good, and the potatoes that came with it were the best part of the brunch. The potato waffle was bland, but they have salt and pepper on the table.

Flash to a couple nights later after a terrible experience at a late-1800's "themed" bar down the street, and The Smith saved our evening. They were able to sit us quickly after we left the prior establishment (given we didn't have a reservation), and their bar was large enough that sitting there while waiting was easy.

Dinner:
Truffle Arancini Appetizer - solid choice, but I'd have liked more of the white sauce.
Half Chicken - the sauce was punchy and was perfect for the chicken and the veggies were cooked perfectly. It wasn't a true half-chicken, but I honestly think it was better because of that. Instead, it was a large breast and a leg; I think this choice kept it from being dried out the way half chickens can be.
French Dip - the au jus was thick and savory, and the bread was amazing. Everything on the sandwich melded together in a great way.
Sticky Toffe Pudding - I've never had such a perfect dessert from a restaurant. It was amazing in every way.

On top of it all, service was great; our waitress, Chloe, was a doll. This was a great experience for us.

title: The Smith
name: Alex Domovich
text:
I have mixed feelings about The Smith, but three stars feels fair — maybe even a touch generous. We booked a table for eight for a family brunch to celebrate my wife’s 40th, so expectations were (understandably) high.

Let’s start with what they absolutely get right. The ambiance is fantastic — very 1920s, F. Scott Fitzgerald energy — and it works. The interior design team clearly put serious thought into creating something transportive and polished. It feels intentional, not accidental.

The cocktails? Genuinely phenomenal. The Oaxacan Sunshine was a standout — bright, balanced, and memorable. My wife’s Frozen Pool Party tasted like something straight out of a Death & Co. mixology finals round. Creative, refined, and just plain fun.

The food was also impressive. The menu strikes a nice balance between healthier options and indulgent brunch staples, and everything we ordered delivered both in flavor and presentation.

Which is why it’s frustrating to say the service didn’t live up to the rest of the experience.

We were seated at a table set with visibly dirty dishes — traces of butter still on the plates. I quietly mentioned it to the server. He inspected them, said nothing, removed them… and never brought replacements or acknowledged the miss. No quick apology, no “we’ll fix that right away,” nothing.

It made me wonder how dishes leave the kitchen that way, washed by one person, set by another, without anyone noticing. It felt either like a simple mistake or a breakdown in attention to detail. And on a milestone birthday brunch, that lack of care stands out.

The Smith has the atmosphere, the drinks, and the food to be exceptional. I just wish the service matched the level of everything else.

text: We visited The Smith’s NOMAD location on a Saturday for brunch: make sure to make reservations beforehand, as it’s a popular time! The staff was really attentive and observant, and took the time to make sure our orders came in correctly. While most were correct, one group member specifically mentioned “no bacon” on her sandwich, yet the sandwich arrived with bacon on it. Our server immediately offered to remake the sandwich, which was a plus! I got the grilled chicken sandwich with mixed greens, and the portions are 50-50 for each, on a big plate! The chicken was well-seasoned, but there was so much sauce, that when it arrived, the bottom of the bread was already soggy. It was hard to eat since the bread kept falling apart with the sauce, and wasn’t as sturdy as it could’ve been. I had a delicious drink (mocha with oat milk) and our group had a great time. One unique touch is the sink station at the entrance, where you can quickly wash your hands!

The pancakes were dry and dense, I didn't enjoy them. The French toast was fluffy and flavorful. The jus sandwich was delicious and I highly recommend it, even though it's relatively expensive.

The space is bright, airy, clean, and well decorated. The service was very efficient, they get people out the door quickly, but never feel pushy.

They took us to a table where it turns out they have a huge TV playing FoxNews facing all the diners, just shoved down your face. We ordered and got our food, the prices were very high, I asked for almond milk since I have a dairy allergy, I got regular milk. The food was basic, nothing special and definitely not worth the price, bodega food would have been better. I personally decided to get the New York sandwich, pastrami on rye… I couldn’t take two bites it was so dry it was inedible. My friends tried it too and agreed. So we just paid and left. The wait staff didn’t notice or seem to care and never asked if the food was good or if we needed anything else like a glass of water with our coffees to attempt to wash down dry and flavorless food.

Just a waste of a NYC meal. Seriously, there are so many better options out there.

I ordered the burger, which was juicy and full of flavor, and the mac and cheese was rich, creamy, and incredibly satisfying. Even the salad was fresh and well prepared. With such a diverse menu, there’s plenty left to explore, and we’ll definitely be back.

I'm a huge martini lover, and one of my favorite things about The Smith is their martini menu where you can get great options for just $12. I had the Salt & Vinegar and the Dirty Blue, which came with fresh blue cheese–stuffed olives that they actually stuff in-house, not store-bought. My wife had oysters, which she absolutely loved.

The service was spectacular and the space was open and comfortable. I've also been here a few times for lunch and always enjoyed the food. All around, a great restaurant that gets it right every time.

text:
Edit:

Went back with some friends and the restaurant was so hot and uncomfortable inside. The food was very good, but it was super hot and loud. We could barely talk. The other issue we had is that the waiter took forever to bring the check, regardless of us asking him for it. I had to stand up and walk towards the register to have the bill paid while the waiter was standing there chatting with 2 other waiters at the moment I gave him my card to pay. I’ve been to 3 different The Smith locations and this is the only one I’ve had bad experiences so far. Not considering coming back to this one again.

We went on a Saturday night without a reservation and got an outdoor table quickly. The waiter was friendly but seemed to be handling over 15 tables alone, which caused a delay when paying. The Smith burger was good, though better on a previous visit, but the ricotta gnocchi was the best I’ve had. Overall, great food, with the gnocchi being a standout.

title: Le Bernardin
name: Anne J.
text: Le Bernardin delivers classic French fine dining with a strong focus on seafood. The dishes are beautifully presented and highlight very fresh, high-quality ingredients. The lobster cappuccino was a standout and a big hit with our group. I would have loved to see a few more meat options, but overall it’s an elegant experience and a great choice for anyone who enjoys refined seafood cuisine.

title: Le Bernardin
name: S Miyata
text:
Wanted to love this so badly as it was a bucket list experience for us. The food was very good, but simply not worth the price tag.

It made me think about what I would expect out of a meal that costs $1000 for two (even without the wine pairing), and what this meal was missing which made it feel so expensive for what it was.

The two things for me were 1) innovation + diversity in courses and 2) complexity in flavor.

The tasting menu started out really strong, but the last 4 savory courses (out of 8) were slabs of white fish or prawn accompanied by varying sauces. The flavors didn’t feel new or very layered/complex. A series of straightforward, solid dishes that mainly rely on the ingredients to shine.

I could see this place being fantastic for those that typically don’t love seafood, or are stopping by for lunch.

The dessert and service were fantastic though!

title: Le Bernardin
name: Michelle Cox
text:
Dining at Le Bernardin was truly exceptional. Every dish was phenomenal, but the tuna with foie gras was particularly memorable - perfectly balanced, luxurious, and beautifully executed. The fish throughout the meal was cooked with incredible precision; each course showcased such delicacy and care that it was clear we were experiencing something very special.

The bread course was also amazing—thoughtfully presented and absolutely delicious, setting the tone for the rest of the evening.

What elevated the experience even further was the service. It was easily some of the best we have ever had. Lehman, Marcos, and Michael were wonderful—warm, attentive, knowledgeable, and genuinely gracious. They made the entire evening feel seamless and welcoming.

We left genuinely impressed by how beautifully everything was prepared and by the extraordinary hospitality. Thank you for such a memorable meal.

title: Le Bernardin
name: lmaltenfort
text:
I am visiting from Colorado, and my friend was visiting from Texas. My parents are regular visitors of this restaurant. My parents told me that we could go sit at the bar and we don’t need reservations. I called the Restaurant at 4 PM and asked if we could come in and sit at the bar… They said absolutely “yes…No reservations needed.”

Restaurant opens at 5 PM. We got there at 6 PM. We were told that someone bought out the wine bar and that the restaurant was not able to accommodate anyone in the lounge or the bar. There were three open seats at the bar, but apparently they were now “Reserved.”

Either a restaurant has principles and directions on what they do and what they don’t do… For example, you do not need reservations at the bar and it’s all open seating. Or that’s not the case. Either way, the person answering the phone should’ve known that the wine bar was bought out and that they were going to reserve seats at the bar… Or they should not have reserved seats at the bar and followed their principles and let anyone walk in.  That is not what is expected from a three Michelin star restaurant

Anyway, we had a great dinner at a neighboring restaurant in the vicinity. I’ve been to many Michelin star restaurants… I doubt I will try to go back here.

title: Le Bernardin
name: Sam Vader
text:
The Good
The dining room is elegant without being oppressive. It strikes that careful balance between refinement and comfort — though parts of it feel just slightly dated, like a luxury hotel that hasn’t refreshed the carpet since its last Michelin ceremony.
Most dishes were beautifully presented. Plates arrived composed and deliberate, each element clearly told where to stand. The cold crab cake was excellent — pristine, delicate, and disciplined. The lobster appetizer showcased sweet, tender lobster that deserved applause.
My daughter’s Peruvian chocolate cake was divine — rich, layered, and unapologetically indulgent. It briefly restored faith in the trajectory of the evening.

The Bad
The tomato broth accompanying the lobster appetizer was aggressively salted. Not enhanced. Not seasoned with confidence. Salted.
The mushroom pasta, while flavorful, lacked the elegance one expects at this level. Presentation matters here.
The main-course lobster in red wine sauce suffered from the same heavy hand. My daughter’s pasta entrée followed suit. By the midpoint of the meal, we began to anticipate the sodium more than the seafood. The pattern was unmistakable: excess salt, consistently applied.
My own dessert, in contrast to my daughter’s triumph, was immensely forgettable — the culinary equivalent of elevator music.

The Ugly
The bread service was the evening’s most surprising miscalculation.
Three varieties were offered. The croissant-like brioche was respectable — soft, buttery, and the sole member of the basket that met expectations.
The focaccia was dry and hard rather than crusty, as though it had been holding a reservation of its own since the afternoon. The petite baguette was indistinguishable from a competent grocery store offering. I have had better bread in diners — and I do not say that lightly.
The assortment of candies presented at the end of the meal was similarly underwhelming. Perfectly edible. Entirely ordinary. At this level, ordinary feels extravagant in the wrong direction.

Final Thought
When dining at this level, one hopes for restraint, refinement, and quiet confidence — not a heavy-handed reminder that salt, like power, is best used judiciously.

title: Le Bernardin
name: Levon Hetki
text: So many years, always BD at Le Bernadine, it always gives the happiness for a bd, with the best ever service, unique atm, and tge menu the food and the tastes combinations surprises - the appetizers and oysters - super start

title: Le Bernardin
name: Mary Beth French
text:
My God! My husband and I just had an amazing French cuisine dinner at Le Bernardin in Midtown N.Y.C.
We felt we were in Paris with the Parisen frescoes on the walls, Candle light on every table, even in the bathrooms. Splendor was our evening.
We had a four course meal. Filet of Sole to start, with a petite bowl of French pasta in a creamy white truffles sauce. The French loaf of bread was fresh out of the oven, the butter from the South of France, made exclusively for Le Bernardin. The atmosphere, the French music, and a live piano player playing into the late hours. Most people there are I’d guess were international, Parisian, and
American. The beautiful women dressed with Valentino, Chanel couture, the finest of the fine. The men were simply too handsome for words. Tuxedos of a variety of colors, black was definitely my favorite.
Famous stars were there eating secretly in a corner tables. Lastly for desert, crème brûlée and double espresso.
Yes, we’re going dancing at Club Alexandra on Park Ave. and 52nd Street, it is a Parisen night in Manhattan. Le Bernardin always
a night to live fully.  My night and food never to forget! Merci, Le Bernardin!

title: ZAZA
name: Vee Velagic
text:
Zara’s on Greenwich St is always a go-to spot for me whenever I’m in New York City. I’ve had breakfast here multiple times, and it never disappoints—good food, consistent quality, every single visit. I honestly haven’t come across anything on the menu I didn’t enjoy.

The staff runs a tight ship—courteous, quick, and efficient, which makes the whole experience smooth. The menu itself is huge, so no matter what you’re in the mood for, you’ll find something. The place is also kept spotless, and you can tell they take pride in it—just check out how clean their restrooms are.

Portions here are generous, so you won’t be leaving hungry. And the coffee? Easily some of the strongest I’ve ever had—exactly how I like it in the morning. I haven’t tried lunch or dinner yet, but judging by the menu, I’d bet they’re just as solid.

Location is also a plus—it’s just steps from the World Trade Center. If you’re in the area, I definitely recommend stopping by.

title: The Smith
name: Bryce W
text: Our waiter was a young white male with blonde hair parted to the side, and his service was absolutely terrible and disrespectful. The only time he came to our table was for our order and the check. We signaled for him several times as he served other tables and it was so apparent it almost seemed as if he were intentionally ignoring us. As POC, it was distressing to see him attend to other tables with white guests and provide our table with very poor service. When the bill came, he purposely peered over to pressure us into giving a tip. The Smith, you are a fine dining institution but you seriously need to reconsider the service of this particular waiter, because it borderlined on discrimination. Embarassing.

title: The Smith
name: Beverly Harper
text: On Dec. 26th, my family and I chose The Smith as our dining spot before attending the Broadway musical Ragtime (which is by the way an amazing must see).  We had reservations of course, and were seated within 5 minutes of arriving. The atmosphere was lively with conversation, and holiday cheer. Our server, Lovell M, was patient, knowledgeable, and attentive - which made the experience relaxing and enjoyable. We were all recovering from the Christmas Day meal, so we ordered lightly. I had the curry spiced branzino with scallion fried rice, as opposed to the 12 ounce double pork chop I really wanted. I chose the former because I was sure no one wanted to smell my leftovers, nor hear me snoring during the play😄😄. The branzino was perfect. It’s Christmas, so yes I had dessert. The honey crisp apple pie, with a toasted oat crumble, and rich vanilla ice cream was absolutely delicious 😋.  We also shared raspberry sorbet. I have never tasted such a sweet, tart, creamy raspberry sorbet in my life. I’m totally ruined now for any other sorbet. Thank you to The Smith for its delicious food, and Lovell M for your outstanding service. I hope to visit again ☺️

title: The Smith
name: Bob
text: While there was concern to begin, as our reservation was not ready and we were forgotten immediately as we left the hostess stand to wait, The Smith quickly redeemed itself as a place that we would love to go back to. The service was rapid, well timed and extremely attentive. The atmosphere was also very nice, good for all moods and types of meals. And the food. Some of the best I’ve had in NYC. Started off with an incredibly flavorful burrata, and followed it up with a perfectly cooked Smith Burger and the extremely balanced short rib pasta. The mains were absolutely stellar, and the apple pie to finish was a perfect sweet cap to our meal. Absolute 5/5 on the food, and regards to the chef for that. The only downside id warn someone looking to attend is the price. The food wasn’t too badly priced, expensive but not anything to balk at in this economy. However, charging 8:50 for a lemonade is borderline price gouging. Would avoid drinks if looking to save, as in our opinion that was absurd. Overall, a great meal and experience; and will absolutely be back next time we are in the city.

title: Le Bernardin
name: Shoban Baskaran
text:
I took my wife for her birthday. We were pretty disappointed.

We got the tasting menu. There was a bunch of courses about 8 I think. Each one was trying to highlight a single piece of seafood.

Honestly very underwhelming all the flavours were muted and boring. The menu feels like it hasn't changed in 10 years. Maybe this menu would have been a hit in a time when finding quality ingredients was difficult.

I did not find any creative aspects to the dishes. With simple burblanc or soy reduction to finish dishes. Paired with a quick sear or poach of a not so unique piece of seafood. I found that the flavours were nothing new and exciting, like something I've tasted a thousand times before.

Service was great as to be expected. Atmosphere again felt like the early 2000.

After 8 courses I was underwhelmed and unsatisfied. And after a short walk through time square I needed to buy another meal from a street shawarma spot. Which is sad because it was so much more.  I know things have changed since I've been there so I'm not sure if they have turned things around. But I still paid for my full meal and expected an experience for what I paid.

This is not a meal we can afford regularly and the meaning of three stars is to plan a whole journey around the meal. From my experience I am disappointed from the restaurant and Michelin. This is a 1 star at best.

Maybe michelin hasn't been back since they gave them their stars 20 years ago.

title: Le Bernardin
name: Henry Kramarski
text:
I will start with saying that, having been to Blue the Cayman Islands from Ripert, I am beyond shocked to say that I feel I got far more in terms of what we paid down there than at Le Bernardin.

The food itself was great, but nothing I would necessarily argue would justify the 3 stars in its entirety. That, I would feel, is brought by the service and attention, which was severely lacking.

Most Michelins I have been to make it a point to focus and excel on service and personal attention, which was sorely lacking here. Minimal efforts made to engage with us, no rounding, plating asynchronous with the rest of the guests. Even small things like a little take home box of chocolates or dessert bites are non-existent. It seems like trivial aspects, but part of why I go to restaurants like this are to feel like they want me to be there, which never once came across.

Contrast this to places like Da Vittorio in Italy, which went above and beyond for 4 of us, and is probably my most memorable 3 star meal to date, and well worth the cost. Cebo, as well as Saddle, both in Madrid and 1 star designations, were much better in my opinion.

Am I happy we went? Yes, its one of the places I felt we needed to try. But can't say I will make it a point to go back.

text: Amazing dining experience deserving of the stars, took advantage of the holiday to try the prix fixe menu. Warm variety of fresh baked bread, decided to go with Shellfish Medley followed by Halibut with Chowder Emulsion and Mont Blanc. The service was seamless, orderly and quick between courses with disturbing conversation.

text:
Le Bernardin — A Michelin Masterclass in Seafood
Dining at Le Bernardin is often described as a culinary pilgrimage for seafood lovers — a place where French technique and ultra-refined seafood take center stage. The experience begins with an elegant Midtown setting and impeccably dressed service team, setting the tone for an unforgettable evening immersed in dining precision.
